meaning: adj.朦胧的,烟雾弥漫的,模糊的
examples: My vision has grown so hazy.
My memories of childhood are hazy and episodic.
The air was thin and crisp, filled with hazy sunshine and frost.
Hence our discoveries are perilously partial, their full consequences for the future hazy at best.
This implication of a certain hazy foreignness about Obama isn't new for Romney .
